data.prompt_text: Two radar stations at B and C are 179 kilometers apart along a straight coastline. They track a ship at sea at point A. The angle \angle B is measured as 81.0^\circ and \angle C is 65.8^\circ. Find the distance from station B to the ship (distance AB).
data.solution_steps:
\text{We are given side } a = 179 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 81.0^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 65.8^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 81.0^\circ - 65.8^\circ = 33.2^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(65.8^\circ)} = \frac{179}{\sin(33.2^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{179\sin(65.8^\circ)}{\sin(33.2^\circ)}

c \approx 298.2

c = \frac{163\sin(29.0^\circ)}{\sin(119.9^\circ)}

c \approx 91.2

\text{We are given side } a = 148 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 119.9^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 17.1^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 119.9^\circ - 17.1^\circ = 43.0^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(17.1^\circ)} = \frac{148}{\sin(43.0^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{148\sin(17.1^\circ)}{\sin(43.0^\circ)}

c \approx 63.8

\text{We are given side } a = 266 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 100.4^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 36.5^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 100.4^\circ - 36.5^\circ = 43.1^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(36.5^\circ)} = \frac{266}{\sin(43.1^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{266\sin(36.5^\circ)}{\sin(43.1^\circ)}

c \approx 231.6

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(35.2^\circ)} = \frac{185}{\sin(73.3^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{185\sin(35.2^\circ)}{\sin(73.3^\circ)}

c \approx 111.3

data.prompt_text: Two hikers at points B and C are 288 feet apart on a flat plain. They are looking at a landmark at A. They measure \angle B = 65.0^\circ and \angle C = 69.9^\circ. What is the distance from hiker B to the landmark (distance AB)?

c = \frac{288\sin(69.9^\circ)}{\sin(45.1^\circ)}

c \approx 381.8
